skill.name: animejs
skill.description: Guides authors who use Anime.js inside HyperFrames compositions. It explains deterministic setup, timeline registration, seek-driven playback, validation commands, and safe boundaries for render-stable animations.
skill.summary: Build deterministic Anime.js animations for HyperFrames compositions.
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many backtick and URL patterns, but the reviewed file is documentation with HTML and command examples. No prompt injection, credential access, data exfiltration, or malicious execution intent was found. The remaining risk is low because copyable examples reference external CDN resources and validation commands.

low_findings.title: External command detections are documentation examples
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE for the repeated Ruby and shell backtick detections. The flagged content is markdown inline code, HTML examples, or two explicit HyperFrames validation commands, with no hidden execution or user-controlled interpolation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are visible documentation examples or literal API names. The only shell commands are fixed validation commands and do not include variables, downloads, or execution chains.

low_findings.title: External CDN and documentation URLs in examples
low_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE for hardcoded URL detection, but severity is low. The examples load Anime.js from jsdelivr and link Anime.js documentation, with no evidence of secret exfiltration or tracking behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are directly visible and serve documented Anime.js resources. They create a supply-chain and availability consideration if copied, but no malicious network intent is evident.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm detection is unsupported
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged metadata description on line 3 discusses Anime.js adapter patterns and contains no cryptographic algorithm, hashing code, or security-sensitive transformation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is plain frontmatter text describing animation usage. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or any cryptographic operation in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance detections are unsupported
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Line 16 warns users not to depend on network state, and line 95 introduces an Avoid section. Neither location performs host inspection, environment probing, or system discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is prose guidance, not executable logic. No reconnaissance commands or APIs appear at those locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Copyable examples reference third-party network resources
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes copyable HTML that fetches Anime.js from jsdelivr. This is common documentation practice, but users may need local vendoring or pinned integrity controls for restricted production builds.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The CDN imports are explicit and easy to verify. The risk is contextual because the skill itself does not execute them, but users could copy them into render code.

content.value_statement: Anime.js animations can drift when they run on their own clock. This skill shows deterministic HyperFrames patterns for registration, seeking, timelines, and validation.
actual_capabilities: Explains the HyperFrames contract for Anime.js ownership and seek-driven playback.
Shows how to disable autoplay for deterministic animation timing.
Demonstrates registering animations and timelines on window.__hfAnime.
Provides HTML script and ES module examples for Anime.js usage.
Recommends HyperFrames lint and validation commands after composition edits.
limitations: Does not include a runtime adapter implementation.
Requires an existing HyperFrames composition context.
CDN examples may need local packaging for offline or locked-down builds.
Recommends GSAP for complex scene sequencing unless Anime.js is required.

use_cases.title: Add deterministic micro-animations
use_cases.description: Create small DOM or SVG animations that HyperFrames can seek reliably during rendering.

use_cases.title: Convert Anime.js examples
use_cases.description: Adapt existing Anime.js snippets into render-safe HyperFrames code with explicit registration.

use_cases.title: Review animation timing
use_cases.description: Check a composition for autoplay, infinite loops, async setup, and validation gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a simple Anime.js animation for a HyperFrames composition. Use deterministic timing, disable autoplay, and register it on window.__hfAnime.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt an existing snippet
prompt_templates.scenario: Convert general Anime.js code into HyperFrames-safe code.
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this Anime.js example for HyperFrames. Make it seek-driven, remove wall-clock behavior, and explain any changes needed for validation.

prompt_templates.title: Build a timeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an Anime.js timeline for a HyperFrames scene with title, accent, and chip animations. Use finite durations and explicit registration.

prompt_templates.title: Audit animation reliability
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this HyperFrames composition that uses Anime.js. Identify autoplay, async setup, infinite loop, network-state, and validation risks.

output_examples.input: Add a seek-driven Anime.js animation to move and fade a mark.
output_examples.output: A concise implementation plan that disables autoplay, creates the animation during initialization, registers it on window.__hfAnime, and validates deterministic playback.

output_examples.input: Review an Anime.js timeline before rendering.
output: Autoplay should be disabled for every animation object.
All timelines should be registered explicitly.
Durations and repeats should be finite.
Validation should run after the composition is edited.

best_practices: Create Anime.js instances synchronously during composition initialization.
Register every animation or timeline on window.__hfAnime.
Run HyperFrames lint and validation after editing animation code.
anti_patterns: Depending on anime.running instead of explicit registration.
Creating animations inside timers, promises, event handlers, or async asset callbacks.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps authors use Anime.js inside HyperFrames while keeping animation playback deterministic and seek-driven.

faq.question: Does it replace the HyperFrames Anime.js adapter?
faq.answer: No. It documents adapter usage patterns and does not provide runtime adapter code.

faq.question: Why should autoplay be disabled?
faq.answer: Autoplay lets Anime.js advance on its own clock. HyperFrames needs to control time for reproducible rendering.

faq.question: How should animations be registered?
faq.answer: Push each returned animation or timeline into window.__hfAnime during composition initialization.

faq.question: Can it use ES module imports?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented pattern works when the returned object exposes seek, pause, and preferably play methods.

faq.question: When should GSAP be used instead?
faq.answer: Use GSAP for complex scene sequencing unless the user specifically requests Anime.js.
